name: Christopsomo Tis Kirias Zinis
recipeInstructions:
-----------------------STARTER DOUGH-----------------------
4 packets Active dry yeast
1/2 teaspoon Sugar
1/2 cup -Hot water, just above body temperature
4 cups All-purpose flour
Combine the semolina flour, sugar, cinnamon, anise, coriander, and cloves in a bowl. Add finely grated zest of oranges and tangerines. Squeeze out juice from oranges and tangerines to obtain 1 1/4 cups juice. Use half this juice to soak currants and raisins for at least 1 hour, preferably overnight. In remaining juice, heat olive oil until almost boiling. Pour into bowl with semolina and spice mixture, stirring. Cover and leave overnight.
Next day, combine dried yeast with sugar and hot water in a cup. Add flour to a bowl, making well in center. Pour yeast mixture in. Squeeze 2/3 cup orange and tangerine juice (warmed), add it. Work into smooth dough, adding more water or warm juice if necessary. Place dough in oiled bowl, cover with cloth, and let rise until nearly doubled in size - about an hour.
Punch down dough in bowl. Combine drained currants and raisins with semolina and spice mixture, working with hands. Combine with dough. Turn onto floured surface, kneading 10 minutes, adding more flour if sticky. Divide dough into three equal pieces. Oil 3 loaf pans (1-1/2 quart capacity). Shape each to fit length of pan, filling corners well. Cover and let rise until nearly doubled in size - another hour.
Bake in preheated oven at 350°F for 1 hour 10 minutes or more; loaves should be rich brown color and make hollow sound when thumped on bottom. Cool on rack. Bread will keep for at least 3 weeks.
